sourceMovie.title: Poor Things
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Poor Things for the same reason it broke through in 2023: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Yorgos Lanthimos keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Safety Not Guaranteed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Poor Things fans keep landing on Safety Not Guaranteed (2012) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Senseless
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Senseless draws from the same science-fiction well as Poor Things, channeling romance in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1998 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004) captures the science-fiction appeal of Poor Things without simply imitating it. The romance streak running through both films is what puts Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Through My Window: Across the Sea
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance appeal of Poor Things — that funny edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into Through My Window: Across the Sea (2023). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Spring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Spring (2014) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Poor Things rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Little Death
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Little Death (2014) is the kind of film Poor Things fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The romance overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Frankenhooker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Frankenhooker shares Poor Things's gift for science-fiction — the 1990 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Fifty Shades Darker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Poor Things compelling — the romance, the drama — shows up in Fifty Shades Darker (2017) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that bittersweet storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Substance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of Poor Things is alive in The Substance (2024), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Lolita
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Lolita (1997) operates in the same romance as Poor Things, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The bittersweet payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

faq.question: What should I watch after Poor Things?
faq.answer: Try Safety Not Guaranteed, Senseless, Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind—each shares DNA with Poor Things while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Poor Things?
faq.answer: Poor Things (2023) is best described as Science Fiction, Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Poor Things worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
